title: mledoze/countries
note: Widely-used, MIT-licensed country metadata dataset (backs many published npm country-data packages). Downloaded the full countries.json directly and extracted Uganda's entry (cca2=UG) with a script, not a summarizer, to avoid truncation (the file is ~1.4MB; an initial WebFetch attempt got cut off before reaching entries starting with 'U'). Source for name/ISO codes/currency/calling code/capital/region/languages/tld/borders/area/demonyms/flag emoji in data/country/uganda.json.

title: File:Flag_of_Uganda.svg
note: Downloaded via the Special:FilePath redirect and visually verified (black/yellow/red bands, grey crowned crane) before use. Public domain per Commons' file page: ineligible for copyright (simple geometric/national symbol) and expired under Uganda's Copyright and Neighbouring Rights Act 2004's non-retrospective provision. Carries a standard 'insignia' usage caveat: national symbol use is independently restricted in many countries regardless of copyright status. Stored at data/country/assets/flag.svg and flag.png (960x640 raster render).

title: File:Coat_of_arms_of_Uganda.svg
note: Downloaded via the Special:FilePath redirect and visually verified (kob antelope + crested crane supporters, sun, drum, spears, 'FOR GOD AND MY COUNTRY' motto banner) before use. Licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 Unported — attribution and share-alike required, which is STRICTER than this project's default CC-BY 4.0 data license (see LICENSE-DATA.md). Stored at data/country/assets/coat-of-arms.svg and coat-of-arms.png (960x1029 raster render).

title: Web search: Uganda time zone and driving side
publisher: Aggregated from timeanddate.com, Wikipedia (Time in Uganda), and driving-in-Uganda travel guides
note: Confirmed Uganda observes East Africa Time (EAT, UTC+03:00) year-round with no DST, and drives on the left (former British colony convention). Both are extremely stable, long-established facts, cross-checked across multiple independent results rather than a single source.

title: Statoids: Districts in Uganda
note: Page states last updated April 2026. Used to cross-check the 135-district count and region groupings.

title: Districts of Uganda
note: Cross-checked district list and region totals (135 as of 2020 baseline, still current at district-count level).

title: List of Local Governments in Uganda
note: Official-style enumerated list of 135 districts; used as the primary cross-check against the other two sources.

title: Counties of Uganda
note: Wikitext table mechanically parsed (see data/legacy/provenance/parse_counties.mjs). Reflects county/district structure as of ~2015 (167 counties, 23 municipal councils) predating the 2016-2020 wave of new districts. Superseded entirely by src-ec-admin-units-2022's county layer (2022, verified, covers all 136 rural districts) — no longer ingested into the build; kept at data/legacy/counties_2015_statoids.csv for provenance only. One item flagged in an earlier pass as a likely source error — a 'Ik County' row under Kaabong District that looked like a duplicate/anomaly — was independently confirmed real by the 2022 EC data (Ik County is a genuine, small Kaabong constituency/county), so that flag was wrong and has been retracted; a 'Nakifuma Council' row that doesn't cleanly fit its rowspan group remains unexplained.

title: Uganda - Subnational Administrative Boundaries (COD-AB)
publisher: OCHA / Humanitarian Data Exchange, sourced from UBOS
note: Evaluated as a candidate bulk source. Rejected for subcounty/parish level: only ~208 admin3 and ~1,520 admin4 features nationally, far below the real count of subcounties/parishes, indicating the dataset is stale and not usable below district level.

title: Electoral Commission of Uganda
note: Identified as the deepest authoritative source. Initial WebFetch-tool requests to ec.or.ug pages returned HTTP 403; a direct request with a standard browser user-agent succeeded (see src-ec-admin-units-2022), which is where the actual village-level bulk data and its provenance are documented. Polling-station-level data (50,739 stations reported for 2025/26) is separate from the administrative-unit gazetteer and remains un-ingested.

title: FULL LIST: Govt approves nine new town councils ahead of 2026 elections
note: Reports 9 new town councils effective 2025-07-01. Direct fetch returned HTTP 403 during research, so the specific list of names/districts is UNVERIFIED and intentionally not included in data/ yet. Needs a primary-source re-check.

title: Uganda's Verified Administrative Units, July 2022 (full village-level gazetteer) + Summary of Administrative Units After Verification, June 2022
note: The genuine bulk source this project was missing: a 3,019-page PDF listing every district > constituency > subcounty/town/division > parish/ward > village in Uganda (146 district-equivalent units, 353 constituencies, 2,191 subcounties/towns/divisions, 10,717 parishes/wards, 71,230 villages), plus a companion 4-page summary-statistics PDF with the same totals per district. Direct WebFetch requests to ec.or.ug returned HTTP 403; the files downloaded fine via a direct request with a standard browser user-agent. Parsed with a custom coordinate-aware script (data/legacy/provenance/ec/parse_admin_units.mjs) because plain text extraction destroys the nested table structure. Validated twice: (1) every one of the ~2,198 'TOTAL VILLAGES IN <subcounty>' checksums printed in the source document itself matches the parser's accumulated count, and (2) fully re-aggregated per-district totals (villages, subcounties, parishes, constituencies) match the companion summary PDF exactly for all 146 districts/cities, zero discrepancies. This is what confirmed Terego District was missing from the original 135-district baseline (it is included here as district-equivalent #142 of 146). Promoted to confidence: verified in data/ec/. Caveat: dated July 2022 — does not reflect any 2023-2026 changes (e.g. the reported-but-unverified 2025/2026 new town councils, see src-monitor-town-councils-2025).

title: User-supplied research cross-checking EC constituency data as a current county source (Manafwa/Namisindwa, Nebbi/Pakwach, Nakapiripirit/Nabilatuk, Hoima/Kikuube, Kaabong/Karenga, Kibaale/Kagadi/Kakumiro)
publisher: UBOS National Population and Housing Census drilldown (statistics.ubos.org/nphc/drilldown), Wikipedia district pages, and several Google AI Mode conversation shares (share.google/aimode/...)
note: User-provided research used to test whether EC 'constituency' names (src-ec-admin-units-2022) are usable as current county names outside city/Kampala divisions. Checked against 13 districts: 11 matched exactly, and 2 (Nakapiripirit, Kikuube) showed the EC data has one additional split the cited sources didn't mention (Chekwii -> Chekwii + Chekwii East; Buhaguzi -> Buhaguzi + Buhaguzi East) — a refinement, not a contradiction. This result is what justified the county-promotion logic in scripts/ingest-ec-admin-units.mjs. Caveat: the share.google/aimode links are AI Mode conversation shares, not primary documents, and were not independently re-fetched/verified by the assistant — treated as corroborating signal alongside the UBOS/Wikipedia links, not as a primary source in their own right. The county layer's actual confidence rests on src-ec-admin-units-2022's own two independent validation passes, not on this cross-check alone.

title: UBOS National Population and Housing Census 2024 — sub-region profile reports (list of report titles)
note: Identifies UBOS's current 16 named sub-regions (Acholi, Ankole, Buganda, Bugisu, Bukedi, Bunyoro, Busoga, Karamoja, Kigezi, Lango, Madi, Rwenzori, Sebei, Teso, Tooro, West Nile) plus a separate Kampala Capital City profile — used as the taxonomy for data/subregions.csv and the new subregion_id field on districts/cities. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: every direct WebFetch attempt against ubos.org and statistics.ubos.org failed with a TLS 'unable to verify the first certificate' error from the fetch tool used; the report-title list and the Madi/Rwenzori/Sebei 2024 splits (see src-subregion-district-mapping-2026) were corroborated via web-search-engine snippets quoting/describing those UBOS pages/PDFs, not a direct primary-document fetch. Treat the UBOS attribution here as search-engine-mediated, not independently verified the way src-ec-admin-units-2022 was — a primary-source re-fetch (e.g. from a browser) would strengthen this. FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-21): that primary-source re-fetch happened, for a separate population-data task — a plain `curl` request with a browser User-Agent and `-k` reached ubos.org and statistics.ubos.org fine (see src-ubos-nphc2024-final-report), confirming the original TLS failure was specific to the WebFetch tool's certificate handling, not a real block. The Final Report PDF fetched then independently confirms this subregion taxonomy: its own Table 2.1 (Population Distribution by sub Region) lists exactly these same 17 groups (16 named sub-regions + Kampala Capital City) with 2024 population figures for each — a genuine primary-document confirmation this entry didn't have when first written.

title: District-to-subregion composition (Wikipedia sub-region articles + citypopulation.de aggregation)
note: Used to assign all 136 districts (plus Kampala and the 10 second-generation cities) to one of the 17 UBOS-named subregions in data/subregions.csv / data/districts.csv / data/cities.csv. Every district, including every post-2015 split (e.g. Karenga from Kaabong, Kwania from Apac, Madi-Okollo/Terego from Arua, Obongi from Moyo, Kazo from Kiruhura, Kikuube from Hoima, Rubanda/Rukiga from Kabale, Rwampara from Mbarara, Kassanda/Kyankwanzi/Kyotera from Mubende/Kiboga/Rakai), was found named explicitly in at least one of these sources alongside its parent district in the same subregion group — no district's placement was inferred purely from its parent without direct confirmation. Cross-checked two ways per subregion (a Wikipedia sub-region article's district list against citypopulation.de's independent administrative breakdown); all matched with no contradictions. Confidence: verified, on the same basis the project already uses for region_id (2+ independent, currently-maintained secondary sources in agreement), not on a fetched UBOS primary document — see src-ubos-subregions-2024's caveat. This is inherently a coarser, more contestable classification than administrative boundaries (sub-region membership reflects a historical/cultural majority, not a claim that a district's population is ethnically homogeneous).

title: geoBoundaries — Uganda ADM3 (labeled 'District')
note: USED for data/geo/districts.geojson. Downloaded directly (550KB raw GeoJSON, not a summarizer pass) and diffed all 137 shapeName values against data/districts.csv programmatically: 136/136 districts matched (5 minor spelling variants — Madi Okollo/Ssembabule matched via existing slug/alias handling; Kalanga->Kalangala, Kitagwenga->Kitagwenda, Rubirzi->Rubirizi needed an explicit override, see scripts/ingest-district-boundaries.mjs), plus one extraneous non-district 'Lake Victoria' water-body polygon (dropped). Critically, this 2020-vintage file already includes Terego District — the split that Statoids/Wikipedia/the MoLG list all missed (see the 136-district entry above) — making it a better vintage than HDX's district layer for this specific purpose. Licensed CC0 1.0 (public domain, no attribution required) per geoBoundaries' own per-level metadata API. Sanity-checked post-ingestion: bounding box (29.58-34.99°E, -1.47-4.22°N) and total area (~211,760 km²) match Uganda's known geography, and area is exactly conserved between the district layer and the region layer dissolved from it (see src-region-boundary-dissolve below).

title: geoBoundaries — Uganda ADM1 ('Region')
note: Evaluated, deliberately NOT used. Confirmed via direct fetch to be exactly the 4 regions (e.g. 'Northern Region'), 2017 vintage. Not ingested because it's licensed ODbL 1.0 (share-alike) — a second, stricter license alongside the CC0 district layer — and because dissolving our own district polygons by region_id (see src-region-boundary-dissolve) guarantees the region shapes can never drift out of sync with this project's own region_id assignments in data/districts.csv. Kept as a source-of-record only in case a future cross-check against an independently-drawn region boundary is useful.

title: Region boundaries dissolved from data/geo/districts.geojson
publisher: This project (derived, not an external source)
note: data/geo/regions.geojson is not sourced from anywhere external — it's produced by scripts/build-region-boundaries.mjs, which groups the CC0 district features (src-geoboundaries-uga-adm3) by region_id and unions each group's geometry with @turf/turf (a devDependency used only at data-prep time, never shipped in the published package). Verified lossless: total area before and after dissolve matches to the km². Central and Eastern come out as MultiPolygon (they contain non-contiguous island districts, e.g. Kalangala/Buvuma); Northern and Western are single contiguous Polygons.

title: geoBoundaries — Uganda ADM2 ('County')
note: Evaluated and rejected for county-level boundaries. Per geoBoundaries' own metadata: 151 counties, 2006 vintage — stale and short of this project's own EC-derived 322 counties/constituencies (see src-ec-admin-units-2022) by more than half. No other free source of standalone county polygon geometry was found either; this project's county *data* (names) comes from EC gazetteer text, not from any geometry source. County-level boundaries are not included in this dataset.

title: geoBoundaries — Uganda ADM4 ('Sub-county')
note: Evaluated, not included in this pass (deferred, not rejected outright). 1,521 features, 2019 vintage, CC BY 3.0 IGO, covering roughly 69% of this project's own verified 2,191 subcounties/town councils/divisions — a real improvement over HDX's ~208 (9.5%) but still a meaningful gap. The raw file also exceeds 10MB unsimplified, direct WebFetch of it hit a size cap, and name-matching against data/ec/administrative_units_ec2022.json's subcounty list was not attempted in this pass. If added later, it should likely ship as a separate opt-in asset (like uganda-locale/deep for parish/ward/cell) rather than bundled into the core package, and would need mapshaper-style simplification plus a full name-reconciliation pass first.

title: GADM — Database of Global Administrative Areas, Uganda
note: Evaluated and rejected outright, regardless of geometry quality. GADM's own license page states redistribution or commercial use is not allowed without prior permission — directly incompatible with this project's CC BY 4.0 / CC0 posture. Also suspected (not fully confirmed — GADM's per-level pages are JS-rendered and count wasn't independently opened) to simply repackage the same stale COD-AB source as HDX: its reported ADM3/ADM4 counts (208/1,520) exactly match HDX's.

title: OpenStreetMap WikiProject Uganda — District Boundaries status page
note: A genuine, actively-maintained alternative for district-level boundaries (all 146 district-equivalents self-reported 'Complete' at admin_level=4 as of the page's 2026-03-05 revision), ODbL-licensed. Not used because geoBoundaries' CC0 layer already fully covers this project's 136 districts with a simpler license. Not independently spot-checked polygon-by-polygon — this is a self-reported project-status table, not something this pass validated at the geometry level. The same page states subcounty/county boundaries are 'future work' and lower levels are mapped mostly as point nodes, not polygons — confirms those tiers aren't available from OSM either, for now.

title: Parish Boundaries: Uganda, 2010
publisher: Uganda Bureau of Statistics, hosted by Stanford Earth Sciences Library / AGSL
note: Evaluated and rejected for parish-level boundaries. A real, public-domain UBOS parish polygon shapefile exists here, but it's 2010/2012 vintage — well over a decade before this project's own verified 10,717-parish EC gazetteer figure, from before roughly half of today's districts existed. The catalog page doesn't publish a feature count, so an exact coverage percentage couldn't be computed, but given the district count alone has more than doubled since 2010, well under half of today's parishes are likely represented. Not ingested.

title: "uganda_village_shapefile" (unlicensed GitHub repository)
publisher: GitHub user justinelliotmeyers, surfaced via a June 2026 'Revolutionary GIS' blog post
note: Evaluated and rejected, the only candidate found for village-level geometry at all. Not trusted from the filename alone — the raw .shp/.dbf headers were inspected byte-by-byte directly: shape type 5 (genuine Polygon geometry, not points), 44,034 records, with a real VILLAGE/PARISH/SUBCOUNTY/COUNTY/DISTRICT field structure consistent with authentic UBOS cartographic data. But: 44,034 is only ~62% of this project's own verified 71,230-village count (src-ec-admin-units-2022, July 2022), the repository has no README, license, or description anywhere (an anonymous single-commit upload), and the '2011' vintage implied by the filename could not be independently confirmed — the .dbf's internal last-modified field just reflects a 2026-06-29 re-save, not original survey date. Given no license and material staleness/incompleteness, this is documented as evaluated-and-rejected, the same way this project already treats other real-but-unusable candidates, rather than ingested. No village/cell-level boundary data is included in this dataset.

title: National Population and Housing Census 2024 — Final Report, Volume 1 (Main)
note: USED for data/population/uganda-nphc-2024-population.csv (146 rows: 136 districts + 10 cities, actual final 2024 census counts, not a projection). Every earlier WebFetch-tool request to ubos.org in this project failed with a TLS certificate error; a plain `curl` request with a browser User-Agent and -k succeeded, revealing this was a tool-side TLS chain issue, not a real access block (see data/legacy/provenance/population/README.md for the full fetch story). Extracted Tables 2.4 A-D (Total Population by District and City, Central/Eastern/Northern/Western) using `pdftotext -table` mode (plain `-layout` mode badly misaligned this table's columns; -table extracted every row correctly). Validated three independent ways with zero discrepancies: (1) male+female=total for all 146 rows: exact for every row; (2) cross-checked against the report's own prose ('Wakiso... 3,411,177... Kalangala... 74,411 people'): exact match; (3) summed all 146 rows' male/female/total columns independently and reproduced the report's separately-stated national total (22,314,289 / 23,591,128 / 45,905,417) to the person across all three columns. All 146 names resolved to this project's own district/city ids with zero unresolved and zero missing (the report already uses this project's existing 'Luweero'/'Ssembabule' aliases). Superior to src-hdx-cod-ps-uga-not-used (2023 projection) precisely because it's the actual current census count, per the project's currency-first sourcing standard. No explicit copyright/license statement was found anywhere in the report; treated as public government statistical data consistent with how other UBOS content is handled in this project, but flagged rather than assumed — see LICENSE-DATA.md.

title: Uganda - Subnational Population Statistics
publisher: OCHA / Humanitarian Data Exchange (data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ps-uga), sourced from UBOS
note: Candidate for a district population layer, considered and then superseded — not ingested. 2023-projected population, age/sex disaggregated, national/region/district levels, CSV+XLSX, CC BY-IGO, last updated 2023-11-09, explicitly built on the old 135-district system (would have needed the same Terego reconciliation solved once already for districts.csv). Superseded by src-ubos-nphc2024-final-report once it turned out ubos.org was directly reachable after all (see that entry) — the actual 2024 census count is more current and more authoritative than a 2023 projection, so this dataset was never ingested.

title: WorldPop — Population Counts for Uganda
note: Candidate for a future population layer, not ingested. 100m-resolution gridded, UN-adjusted population raster (GeoTIFF), not tied to any fixed admin schema — would need zonal-statistics GIS processing (e.g. summing raster cells within each district/subcounty polygon) rather than being a drop-in CSV join. More flexible than src-hdx-cod-ps-uga (works with any future boundary layer, not just district) but meaningfully more engineering effort.

title: location.csv (user-supplied legacy dataset)
publisher: Unknown/original provenance not documented by user
note: The project's original seed file (135-row header implies district+county+subcounty, but only covered 110 of 135 districts and the county column duplicates the subcounty name for most rows, i.e. it does not contain real county data). Migrated as-is to data/legacy/subcounties_legacy_source.csv for reconciliation, not treated as verified.
